dataset: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive (Radboud UMC & University of Liverpool Drug Interaction Database)

pi_items.source: NEJM, FDA documentation, clinical trials (COU-AA-302, COU-AA-303) establish abiraterone as standard treatment for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer

All three are opioid analgesics used in cancer pain management. Codeine and Tramadol are CYP2D6-dependent prodrugs; Morphine is independent of CYP2D6 metabolism. All are appropriate for testing with abiraterone (a CYP2D6 inhibitor)
DrugBank, FDA label (202379), in vivo DDI study showing 2.8-2.9 fold increase in dextromethorphan (CYP2D6 substrate) exposure with abiraterone + prednisone

pi_items.metadata_value: Requires CYP2D6 for bioactivation to morphine (active metabolite)
pi_items.verified_value: Codeine is a prodrug requiring CYP2D6-mediated O-demethylation to morphine (active metabolite) for analgesic effect
pi_items.source: NCBI/Medical Genetics Summaries on Codeine Therapy and CYP2D6; CPIC Guidelines; only 5-10% of codeine undergoes this activation pathway to morphine

pi_items.metadata_value: Requires CYP2D6 for bioactivation to active metabolite
pi_items.verified_value: Tramadol is bioactivated by CYP2D6 to O-desmethyltramadol (M1), which is 300-fold more potent than tramadol at the mu-opioid receptor
pi_items.source: NCBI/Medical Genetics Summaries on Tramadol Therapy and CYP2D6; M1 metabolite is up to 6 times more potent in producing analgesia than parent tramadol

pi_items.verified_value: Morphine is primarily metabolized by UGT2B7 (glucuronidation, Phase II metabolism) producing morphine-3-glucuronide and morphine-6-glucuronide. Only minor CYP3A4 involvement for N-demethylation. NOT a CYP2D6 substrate
pi_items.source: NCBI Opioid Metabolism reviews; Oxford Academic (Pain Medicine); morphine is among opioids with fewest drug-drug interactions due to UGT metabolism pathway

pi_items.metadata_value: Abiraterone CYP2D6 inhibition reduces codeine efficacy
pi_items.verified_value: Abiraterone moderately inhibits CYP2D6, resulting in decreased conversion of codeine to active metabolite morphine, thereby reducing analgesic efficacy
pi_items.source: Drugs.com Drug-Drug Interaction Checker; cancer-druginteractions.org (Radboud/Liverpool database); recommendation is to avoid or use alternative drug

pi_items.metadata_value: Abiraterone CYP2D6 inhibition reduces tramadol efficacy
pi_items.verified_value: Abiraterone moderately inhibits CYP2D6, reducing conversion of tramadol to active metabolite M1, thereby reducing analgesic efficacy
pi_items.source: Drugs.com DDI Checker; cancer-druginteractions.org; Liverpool/Uptodate databases warn against coadministration

pi_items.metadata_value: Abiraterone does NOT significantly interact with morphine
pi_items.verified_value: Abiraterone does not significantly affect morphine metabolism or efficacy because morphine is not a CYP2D6 substrate and undergoes glucuronidation instead
pi_items.source: cancer-druginteractions.org; NCBI Opioid Metabolism literature; morphine metabolism via UGT2B7 is not affected by CYP2D6 inhibitors like abiraterone
Pharmacological basis: morphine is unaffected by abiraterone's CYP2D6 inhibition. Clinical practice: morphine is standard for cancer pain management and safer with CYP2D6 inhibitors
Both are CYP2D6-dependent prodrugs. Abiraterone's CYP2D6 inhibition impairs their bioactivation to active metabolites, reducing efficacy
Central to the mechanism: abiraterone is a CYP2D6 inhibitor; codeine and tramadol both require CYP2D6 bioactivation
Key outcome: CYP2D6 inhibition by abiraterone reduces the efficacy of codeine and tramadol, while morphine efficacy is unaffected
Codeine → morphine (active metabolite); Tramadol → O-desmethyltramadol (active metabolite); morphine itself is metabolized to glucuronide conjugates
Codeine and tramadol are prodrugs requiring CYP2D6-mediated bioactivation for their active forms
Both codeine and tramadol are prodrugs: codeine has weak mu-receptor binding and requires conversion to morphine; tramadol is similarly a prodrug requiring M1 activation

pi_items.verified_value: Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive - real drug interaction database from Radboud UMC and University of Liverpool
pi_items.source: cancer-druginteractions.org; app available on Google Play and Apple App Store; database originally at cancer-druginteractions.org (archived after September 2023)
Confirmed in setup_cancer_ichart.sh environment script and Google Play/Apple App Store listings

privileged_info_summary: Abiraterone is a moderate CYP2D6 inhibitor used for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer. Codeine and Tramadol are prodrugs that require CYP2D6-mediated bioactivation to their active metabolites (morphine and O-desmethyltramadol, respectively) for pain relief. When abiraterone inhibits CYP2D6, the bioactivation of both codeine and tramadol is impaired, resulting in reduced analgesic efficacy. In contrast, morphine is not a CYP2D6 substrate—it undergoes glucuronidation (UGT2B7) and is therefore unaffected by abiraterone's CYP2D6 inhibition, making it the preferred opioid recommendation when used concomitantly with abiraterone. The Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive is a legitimate medical drug interaction database maintained by the University of Liverpool and Radboud UMC, containing evidence-based interaction data using a traffic-light severity system.
